# Transcrição: Be_so_good_they_can't_ignore_you!

- **Fonte:** Be_so_good_they_can't_ignore_you!
- **Duração:** 01:03
- **Idioma:** en

---

## [00:00 - 01:03]

I've got a question here from Zai Loves Music and she asks, then I'm curious to know how you would deal with unfair treatment, racial discrimination specifically. I'm an Asian singer here in Australia. Zai, I'm assuming that you're referring to sometimes you don't get booked because of your background. I can relate to this if that's the case because for me there are times I don't get booked as a speaker as a result of my background. And here's what I'll say to you if this is what's currently happening. There's a great quote from a comedian named Steve Martin who says, be so good they can't ignore you. That's what I'm going to say to you is be so good they can't ignore you. Don't focus on the fact that sometimes things aren't fair. Don't focus on the things you can't control. Focus on the things you can control because you can control getting better at your craft. And I know what this means is that it's harder for us but ultimately shift the way you think about it, reframe it. This is a gift. This is an opportunity for you to become the best, for you to become better. Become undeniable at what you do and become so good that they can't ignore you Zai. You got this.
